Heatmap

Cooper Joseph Sharp

2026-03-27

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df  <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/analytic-sample.csv")
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CORRELATION HEATMAPS BY GROUP
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
library(ggplot2)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.3.3
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
library(tidyr)

# ── Prepare full analytic sample (all 3 groups) ──────────────────────────────
dat_all <- df %>%
  mutate(
    PG_group = case_when(
      pg == 0 ~ "Healthy Control",
      pg == 1 ~ "Recreational Gambler",
      pg == 2 ~ "Problem Gambler"
    ),
    gender = factor(demo_gender),
    income = factor(income_median_split),
    z_age           = as.numeric(scale(demo_yrs)),
    z_yoe           = as.numeric(scale(ses_yoe)),
    z_fevs          = as.numeric(scale(fevs_total)),
    z_usidep        = as.numeric(scale(usidep_total)),
    z_reward        = as.numeric(scale(rew_composite)),
    z_dospert_inv   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_inv)),
    z_dospert_ethic = as.numeric(scale(dospert_ethic)),
    z_dospert_soc   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_soc)),
    z_dospert_hs    = as.numeric(scale(dospert_hs)),
    z_dospert_rec   = as.numeric(scale(dospert_rec)),
    z_depression    = as.numeric(scale(susd_depression)),
    z_mania         = as.numeric(scale(susd_mania)),
    z_anxiety       = as.numeric(scale(scared_total)),
    z_loneliness    = as.numeric(scale(uclal_total)),
    z_planfulness   = as.numeric(scale(planfulness_mean)),
    z_neigh_disadv  = rowMeans(cbind(
      as.numeric(scale(TotalCrime)),
      as.numeric(scale(unemployed)),
      as.numeric(scale(gini))
    ), na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(
    PG_group, gender, income,
    z_neigh_disadv, z_age, z_yoe,
    z_reward,
    z_dospert_inv, z_dospert_ethic, z_dospert_soc, z_dospert_hs, z_dospert_rec,
    z_depression, z_mania, z_anxiety, z_loneliness, z_planfulness,
    z_fevs, z_usidep, TotalCrime, unemployed, gini
  ) %>%
  filter(gender %in% c(1, 2))

# ── Variables & clean labels ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
heatmap_vars <- c(
  "z_neigh_disadv", "z_age", "z_yoe",
  "z_reward",
  "z_dospert_hs", "z_dospert_soc", "z_dospert_ethic",
  "z_dospert_inv", "z_dospert_rec",
  "z_depression", "z_mania", "z_anxiety",
  "z_loneliness", "z_planfulness",
  "z_fevs", "z_usidep"
)

var_labels <- c(
  "z_neigh_disadv"  = "Neighbourhood Disadvantage",
  "z_age"           = "Age",
  "z_yoe"           = "Years of Education",
  "z_reward"        = "Reward Sensitivity",
  "z_dospert_hs"    = "DOSPERT: Health/Safety",
  "z_dospert_soc"   = "DOSPERT: Social",
  "z_dospert_ethic" = "DOSPERT: Ethical",
  "z_dospert_inv"   = "DOSPERT: Investment",
  "z_dospert_rec"   = "DOSPERT: Recreational",
  "z_depression"    = "Depression",
  "z_mania"         = "Mania",
  "z_anxiety"       = "Anxiety",
  "z_loneliness"    = "Loneliness",
  "z_planfulness"   = "Planfulness",
  "z_fevs"          = "Financial Vulnerability",
  "z_usidep"        = "Financial Dependence"
)

# ── Helper: compute correlation matrix and reshape to long ───────────────────
get_cor_long <- function(data, vars, label) {
  cor_mat <- cor(data[, vars], use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  cor_mat[upper.tri(cor_mat)] <- NA  # keep lower triangle only
  
  as.data.frame(cor_mat) %>%
    mutate(var1 = rownames(cor_mat)) %>%
    pivot_longer(-var1, names_to = "var2", values_to = "r") %>%
    filter(!is.na(r)) %>%
    mutate(
      var1  = factor(var_labels[var1], levels = rev(var_labels)),
      var2  = factor(var_labels[var2], levels = var_labels),
      group = label
    )
}

# ── Build each group's long correlation data ──────────────────────────────────
cor_hc  <- get_cor_long(filter(dat_all, PG_group == "Healthy Control"),        heatmap_vars, "Healthy Controls")
cor_rg  <- get_cor_long(filter(dat_all, PG_group == "Recreational Gambler"),   heatmap_vars, "Recreational Gamblers")
cor_pg  <- get_cor_long(filter(dat_all, PG_group == "Problem Gambler"),        heatmap_vars, "Problem Gamblers")
cor_all <- get_cor_long(dat_all,                                                heatmap_vars, "Full Sample")

# ── Shared heatmap theme ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
heatmap_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.title   = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid   = element_blank()
  )

heatmap_scale <- scale_fill_gradient2(
  low      = "steelblue",
  mid      = "white",
  high     = "firebrick",
  midpoint = 0,
  limits   = c(-1, 1),
  name     = "r"
)

# ── Plot function ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plot_heatmap <- function(cor_long, title) {
  ggplot(cor_long, aes(var2, var1, fill = r)) +
    geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_text(aes(label = ifelse(abs(r) >= 0.0 & var1 != var2,
                                 sprintf("%.2f", r), "")),
              size = 2.5, colour = "grey20") +
    heatmap_scale +
    heatmap_theme +
    labs(title = title)
}

# ── Generate all 4 plots ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_hc  <- plot_heatmap(cor_hc,  "Healthy Controls")
p_rg  <- plot_heatmap(cor_rg,  "Recreational Gamblers")
p_pg  <- plot_heatmap(cor_pg,  "Problem Gamblers")
p_all <- plot_heatmap(cor_all, "Full Sample")

print(p_hc)

print(p_rg)

print(p_pg)

print(p_all)
